{{Quote|Mal'ary'ush!|Khabarakh|<ref name="Heir to the Empire" />}}
'''Khabarakh''' was a [[Noghri/Legends|Noghri]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] member of [[Clan Kihm'bar]] hailing from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Honoghr/Legends|Honoghr]]. He participated in [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]] [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn's]] [[Thrawn campaign|campaign]] to [[Death/Legends|destroy]] the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], attempting to kidnap [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] during a [[Mission to Kashyyyk (Thrawn campaign)|Mission]] to the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] planet of [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]]. During the mission, his teammates were killed by the planet's inhabitants, [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiees]]. However, when Khabarakh got near his target, his sense of smell allowed him to recognize Solo a ''[[Mal'ary'ush]]'', the "''daughter of the savior''" of the Noghri people, the daughter of [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], who was considered by the Noghri as their savior. The Noghri agent agreed to take Leia to Honoghr, as she had promised him the New Republic would help his [[Species/Legends|species]].

Although [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], a Wookiee agent of the New Republic, had agreed to stay on the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], he changed his mind and insisted on accompanying the princess to the Noghri [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], stating that he had extended his [[Life debt/Legends|life debt]] he owed to Solo's [[Marriage/Legends|husband]], [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] to Organa. Khabarakh agreed, as he believed that the Wookiee code of honor was similar to the Noghri's code of honor. However, Chewbacca's statements were undone after Thrawn's [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]], the [[Chimaera/Legends|''Chimaera'']] appeared over the planet, prompting Chewbacca to assault the Noghri. Later, Noghri was brought to Honoghr, surprising Thrawn, who had thought him dead, and expected him to be a [[Treason/Legends|traitorous]] Noghri. After the Chiss found evidence of possible treason and [[Prison/Legends|imprisoned]] him at [[Nystao]], where he was later freed by Organa, who later freed the Noghri's word from the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire's control]] and helped him rebuild it with other Noghri after [[Battle of Bilbringi (9 ABY)|Thrawn's death]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|Do not worry. I will keep you safe.|Khabarakh to Leia|<ref name="DFR" />}}
Sent by [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] on a [[Mission to Kashyyyk (Thrawn campaign)|mission to capture]] the [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]], he and his teammates in [[Imperial Noghri Commando Team 22]] traveled to [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]]. There his teammates were killed by [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiees]], but when he came close to Leia, his highly acute sense of smell helped him recognize her as ''[[Mal'ary'ush]]'': the biological daughter of Lord [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], whom the Noghri considered their savior. Khabarakh agreed to take Leia, who had promised him the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] would help the Noghri people, to [[Honoghr/Legends|Honoghr]].<ref name="Heir to the Empire" />

[[File:KhabarakhChewbacca.png|thumb|left|200px|Chewbacca assaults Khabarakh in the cockpit of the Noghri's shuttle]]
Although Chewbacca had previously agreed to stay on the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], he changed his mind and insisted on accompanying Leia to Honoghr, as he had extended the [[Life debt/Legends|life debt]] he owed [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], to her and her [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|brother]]. Khabarakh agreed to this, stating that "The [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] code of honor is similar to our own." However, the unexpected appearance of a Star Destroyer sent [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] into a rage, as the Wookiee believed the Noghri had betrayed him and Leia to [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]]. Chewbacca and Khabarakh scuffled in the cockpit for a while, and though Khabarakh was a skilled warrior, Chewbacca's pure strength overwhelmed the Noghri, and he was forced to use a stun weapon to save the lives of his passengers and himself. With Chewbacca subdued Khabarakh sent out the recognition code, albeit late.<ref name="Heir to the Empire" />

His reappearance on Honoghr, however, drew the attention of Thrawn, who thought him dead on Kashyyyk, and believed that he was interrogated and released after divulging some Imperial information. Thrawn wanted to find out exactly what the information was. Khabarakh showed [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] several Noghri styles of combat, teaching him a bit in the lull. Furthering Thrawn's belief that he was interrogated was that [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperials]] found Wookiee hair in his ship. Khabarakh was arrested for [[Treason/Legends|treason]], and was imprisoned in [[Nystao]], but was freed when Leia revealed the Imperials' treachery in poisoning their world.<ref name="Heir to the Empire" />

When [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] came to Honoghr seeking the aid of the Noghri, Khabarakh and [[Ovkhevam]] greeted Luke at a new area of ''clean lands'' and helped him repair his ship. Luke reflected that he didn't really need the help but that the sooner the Noghri people got over their insisted debt to the Skywalkers, the better.<ref name="Heir to the Empire" />

[[File:Khabarakh_EGTAS.jpg|thumb|right|180px|Khabarakh]]
Later, Khabarakh was part of the elite guard, commanded by [[Cakhmaim]], who were chosen to protect Leia and the [[Solo twins (disambiguation)|Solo twins]]. After the death of Thrawn, he returned to his planet to help rebuild it, with the help of the New Republic, as Leia had promised.<ref name="TLC">''[[The Last Command]]''</ref>

At the time of the [[Caamas Document Crisis]], Khabarakh commanded the sub-detail of Leia's honor guard assigned to her children, who now also included the twins' younger brother, [[Anakin Solo]]. These guards accompanied the Solo children during Leia's vacation to [[Wayland/Legends|Wayland]], and later to Kashyyyk when the children were sent there to wait out the Crisis.<ref name="Specter of the Noghri">''[[Specter of the Past]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Khabarakh first appeared in ''[[Heir to the Empire]]'', the first volume in [[Timothy Zahn]]'s ''[[Star Wars: The Thrawn Trilogy]]''.<ref name="Heir to the Empire" />
